channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Record-breaker Dowman scores late as Arsenal beat Everton
item.description: Sixteen-year-old Max Dowman becomes the youngest scorer in Premier League history, as Arsenal score twice in the last 10 minutes to beat Everton 2-0 and move 10 points clear at the top of the table.

item.title: Man City held by West Ham who move out of relegation zone
item.description: West Ham come from behind to earn a point against Manchester City as Nuno Espirito Santo's side move out of the bottom three, while Pep Guardiola's side drop nine points behind leaders Arsenal in the Premier League.

item.title: Gordon goal gives Newcastle rare win at Chelsea
item.description: Chelsea's hopes of qualifying for the Champions League are dealt a blow as Newcastle's Anthony Gordon scores the only goal of the game, to clinch a first away win at Stamford Bridge since 2012 for the Magpies.

item.title: Strange Minteh goal enough to give Brighton win over Sunderland
item.description: Yankuba Minteh scores the only goal of the game in bizarre fashion as Brighton beat Sunderland 1-0 at the Stadium of Light, for their third win in four games in the Premier League.

item.title: Bournemouth held by struggling Burnley
item.description: Bournemouth lost further ground in the race for European football as they were held to a fourth consecutive Premier League draw by relegation-threatened Burnley at Turf Moor.

item.title: Gordon hits back at 'complete nonsense' from Rooney and Shearer
item.description: Newcastle United's match-winner against Chelsea, Anthony Gordon, responds to "complete nonsense" from pundits Wayne Rooney and Alan Shearer.

item.title: How Sancho's dream Man Utd move became a nightmare
item.description: Jadon Sancho is set to leave Manchester United this summer, almost three years after his last Premier League appearance for the club.

item.title: Rosenior says referee should focus on job, not Chelsea huddle
item.description: There was an unusual addition to Chelsea's huddle before their game against Newcastle at Stamford Bridge - referee Paul Tierney.

item.title: Bespoke women's football stadiums? Why US sides are making the move
item.description: BBC Sport visits Kansas City Current and Denver Summit to learn why these US franchises are the first to build venues specifically for women's football.

item.title: Real open to Camavinga offers - Sunday's gossip
item.description: Premier League clubs offered Eduardo Camavinga hope, Arsenal in talks for Castello Lukeba, and Adam Wharton wanted by top-flight trio.

item.title: How history-maker Dowman stole show for Arsenal
item.description: Ever since he pulled on an Arsenal shirt, Max Dowman has been breaking records - and the 16-year-old played a key role in what could prove to be a vital win over Everton.

item.title: Unbalanced Man City cling to faint title hopes as Haaland struggles
item.description: Saturday evening, when Arsenal earned a dramatic late win over Everton before Man City were held at West Ham, felt as if it could be pivotal in the Premier League title race.

item.title: Can you name every Women's League Cup finalist?
item.description: The Women's League Cup final takes place on Sunday as Manchester United take on Chelsea at Ashton Gate. Can you name every finalist in the competition's short history?

item.title: 'They know where I am' - could Redknapp return to manage Spurs?
item.description: Former Tottenham boss Harry Redknapp says the Spurs hierarchy "know where I am" if they decide to replace interim boss Igor Tudor – so could the 79-year-old make a sensational return to management?

item.title: 'I'm as confused as Cole Palmer' - huddle incident baffles MOTD pundits
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Ashley Williams and Joe Hart question why referee Paul Tierney protected the match ball on the centre spot, by getting in the middle of the Chelsea players' pre-match huddle before their Premier League game against Newcastle.

item.title: We've got a special player on our hands with Dowman - Hart
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Joe Hart and Ashley Williams praise Max Dowman after the 16-year old became the youngest goalscorer in the Premier League in Arsenal's 2-0 win over Everton.

item.title: 'Rare to have a baby as a footballer' - Man Utd's Bizet Donnum
item.description: Ellen White sits down with Manchester United forward Celin Bizet Donnum for Mother's Day to talk through the Norwegian's pregnancy journey - from the decision-making process to the support policies in place.

item.title: 'Incredible' Dowman changed 'unbelievable' game - Arteta
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta says Max Dowman "is not fazed" by any occasion as the 16-year old comes off the bench to become the Premier League's youngest goalscorer in Arsenal's 2-0 win over Everton.

item.title: Man City still have 'hope' in title race - Guardiola
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says his side still have "hope" in the title race but admits they lack consistency compared to previous seasons, after their 1-1 draw at West Ham.

item.title: Quiz: Who does Dowman join as Premier League's 10 youngest scorers?
item.description: Arsenal's Max Dowman became the youngest ever player to score a Premier League goal. But who else is on the list?

item.title: 'Sensational' Yang shows impact as Celtic keep believing
item.description: Hyun-jun Yang sparked for Celtic against Motherwell, but how deep has his impact been?

item.title: Which team needs Women's League Cup final win more?
item.description: Chelsea and Manchester United will battle to lift the Women's League Cup trophy on Sunday - but who needs the success more?

item.title: Three more Iran women's players change minds over asylum
item.description: Four of seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

item.title: How Newcastle took advantage of James playing in midfield
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Joe Hart explains how Newcastle were able to take advantage of Chelsea playing Reece James in midfield instead of his usual right-back position.

item.title: Referee disrupting pre-match huddle is 'disappointing' - Rosenoir
item.description: Chelsea manager Liam Rosenoir says his team's huddle in the centre circle of the pitch is a "show of respect" to each other rather than to annoy the opposition, after referee Paul Tierney disrupts the pre-match ritual in Chelsea's 1-0 defeat to Newcastle.

item.title: Arsenal defeat 'absolutely cruel' on Everton - Moyes
item.description: Everton manager David Moyes says his side have shown how much they have improved this season, despite being beaten late on by Arsenal at the Emirates Stadium.

item.title: West Ham were 'heroic' to earn point against Man City - Nuno
item.description: West Ham manager Nuno Espirito Santo praises his side's defensive display, stating his players "put their bodies on the line" to earn a 1-1 draw against Manchester City at the London Stadium.

item.title: Chelsea win a confidence boost for Barcelona tie - Howe
item.description: Newcastle manager Eddie Howe believes his side have built momentum for their Champions League last-16 second leg against Barcelona with a 1-0 victory against Chelsea at Stamford Bridge in the Premier League.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v YouTuber Angry Ginge
item.description: BBC Sport football expert Chris Sutton takes on YouTuber Angry Ginge plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's Premier League fixtures on Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday.

item.title: Police probe fan altercation at Emirates after Barry claims
item.description: Police are investigating an altercation in the away section at Emirates Stadium after Everton striker Thierno Barry claimed his friends were "attacked" during Saturday's 2-0 defeat by Arsenal.

item.title: Iraq taking private plane to Mexico for World Cup play-off
item.description: Iraq will travel to Mexico by private plane for their World Cup play-off despite calls from coach Graham Arnold to postpone the match amid the escalating US-Israeli war with Iran, the country's FA president Adnan Dirjal says.

item.title: Iraola frustrated by wasteful Bournemouth in Burnley draw
item.description: Bournemouth manager Andoni Iraola believes his side had "more than enough chances to score" and criticises the decision not to award striker Evanilson a penalty, as Bournemouth settle for a 0-0 draw with Burnley at Turf Moor.

item.title: A game of fine margins - Parker on Bournemouth draw
item.description: Burnley manager Scott Parker says his side remain focused and continue to work hard to avoid relegation, as The Clarets settle for a 0-0 draw at home to Bournemouth in the Premier League.

item.title: Cross or shot? Brighton's Minteh reacts to win at Sunderland
item.description: Yankuba Minteh is interviewed by Final Score's Jason Mohammed after the Brighton forward scores the winning goal against Sunderland in the Premier League.

item.title: Sunderland showed great mentality despite Brighton defeat - Le Bris
item.description: Sunderland manager Regis Le Bris hopes his side will "learn alot" from the "relentless" nature of the Premier League, as the Black Cats suffer their third consecutive home defeat, losing 1-0 to Brighton.

item.title: Brighton showed great togetherness to win - Hurzeler
item.description: Brighton manager Fabian Hurzeler believes his side "controlled the game" in a "deserved" win as they beat Sunderland 1-0 at the Stadium of Light and move into the top half of the Premier League table.

item.title: Sunderland need to stay hungry - director of football Ghisolfi
item.description: Sunderland's director of football Florent Ghisolfi joins the Football Focus team at the Stadium of Light to talk about the club's objectives in the short and long term.

item.title: Brentford, the World Cup and an itch to return to Gaelic football
item.description: Brentford and Republic of Ireland captain Nathan Collins tells Kelly Somers about his dreams of the World Cup.

item.title: Premier League quiz: Name Championship play-off winners who stayed up
item.description: Sunderland went up as Championship play-off winners last season and look set for Premier League survival this campaign.

item.title: Stoger stunner helps Monchengladbach to crucial win against St Pauli
item.description: Kevin Stoger scores a stunning free-kick to help Borussia Monchengladbach ease their relegation concerns as they comfortably beat St Pauli 2-0 in the German Bundesliga.

item.title: What's it like to be a Sunderland fan?
item.description: Sunderland fan and BBC Sport commentator Steve Cram charts the highs and lows of supporting the Black Cats, from the depths of League One to the dizzy heights of the Premier League.

item.title: 'Simply stunning' - Monchengladbach take lead against St. Pauli in style
item.description: Kevin Stoger's first goal of the season is one to remember as he gives Borussia Monchengladbach a stunning lead against St. Pauli in the Bundesliga.

item.title: 'You can be the victim or change' - Tudor on Spurs mentality
item.description: Tottenham interim boss Igor Tudor says his players can either "cry or fight" as they try and avoid relegation from the Premier League.

item.title: 'I do what's best for the team' - Guardiola on Real Madrid defeat
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola defends his team selection for their 3-0 defeat by Real Madrid in the Champions League last-16 first leg.

item.title: Owen 'can't believe' some pundits aren't backing Carrick
item.description: Michael Owen has said he "can't believe people are questioning" whether Michael Carrick should become the permanent manager of Manchester United.

item.title: Too good to go down? Ranking shock Premier League relegations
item.description: Premier League champions, FA Cup winners and club legends have all suffered the unwanted fate, but which relegated side really was "too good to go down"?

item.title: Players must believe in your methods - the mentality of winning a relegation battle
item.description: In his BBC Sport column, former Premier League boss Tony Pulis explains how your mindset is as important as your tactics when you are a manager battling relegation.

item.title: The 24 hours that damaged the Premier League's best-in-the-world reputation
item.description: Phil McNulty examines the state of the Premier League after none of its six Champions League entrants won this week.

item.title: From Norfolk to La Masia - Tavares is England's one to watch
item.description: Family, ex-coaches and former team-mates of Ajay Tavares describe the 16-year-old's rise from a small Norfolk market town to FC Barcelona.

item.title: Will Everton's formidable away form throw open title race?
item.description: BBC Sport examines whether Everton can win for a fifth time in six away games at Premier League leaders Arsenal.

item.title: Tudor's tactics: Pinpointing how his choices have made things worse
item.description: After Tottenham's Champions League collapse on Tuesday, the appointment of Igor Tudor and the tactics employed increasingly seems to have made their situation worse.

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What happens when a manager gets suspended?
item.description: Ever wondered what a touchline ban entails? BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain what punishment managers actually face.

item.title: Why are there two 5.30pm Premier League kick-offs on Saturday?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains why where are a pair of 5:30pm kick-offs in the Premier League on Saturday

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: Which club has the most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the record for most red cards in a Premier League season

item.title: What is St Totteringham's Day and when could it happen?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains what St Totteringham's Day is and what its origins are

item.title: VAR vs TMO – what are the key differences?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the key differences between football's video assistant referees and rugby union's use of television match officials.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League appearances?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything Team look at which players have the highest total Premier League appearances.

item.title: 'It's a start' - Robinson strives for stability as Aberdeen tenure begins
item.description: Stephen Robinson took charge of Aberdeen for the first time, but what can he take away from the draw with Falkirk?

item.title: Dundee derby, Chermiti & Rohl in spotlight - Sunday's games in focus
item.description: The Dundee derby, Rangers striker Youssef Chermiti and manager Danny Rohl are all in the spotlight in Sunday's Scottish Premiership games.

item.title: Butland open to extending Rangers stay - gossip
item.description: Goalkeeper Jack Butland is open to extending his Rangers contract as Heart of Midlothian will reportedly reject any Celtic approach for Claudio Braga.

item.title: Masked fan unplugs VAR monitor in German second division match
item.description: A masked fan unplugged a VAR monitor in a German second division match as the referee went to the pitchside screen to check a penalty award.

item.title: Quiz: One question for each team in the Champions League last 16
item.description: The Champions League is back, but how much do you know about each of the 16 teams still in the competition?

item.title: Pigeon stops play - unflappable Gimenez removes intruder
item.description: A pigeon briefly stopped play during Atletico Madrid's 3-2 win over Real Sociedad - with unflappable defender Jose Gimenez plucking the bird off the pitch and taking it to safety.

item.title: No fear - so can Man Utd finally overcome nemesis Chelsea?
item.description: Manchester United have not enjoyed recent finals against Chelsea, but can they finally overcome their nemesis in Sunday's Women's League Cup final?

item.title: Ex-England women's youth player Carr dies aged 35
item.description: The England women's football team pays tribute to former youth player Amy Carr, who has died at the age of 35.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?

item.title: When River Plate icon Diaz took over fourth-tier Oxford
item.description: Twenty years on from Ramon Diaz's surprising appointment at Oxford United, BBC Sport looks at what happened and how it went.
